title: rottlera hookeriana
intro: Rottlera hookeriana, also known as the Rottlera tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries like India and Sri Lanka. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a compound called flavonoids, that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rottlera hookeriana include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. The plant is used to prepare herbal remedies in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are typically administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
